metadata.title: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Experimental study with hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
metadata.demographics: Ten male and ten female subjects, age 18-35 years, Caucasian race, BMI 20-25 kg/m2, no excessive sport activities, no medication

evidence.quote: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
TheJournalofClinicalEndocrinology&Metabolism92(9):3646–3652
Copyright©2007byTheEndocrineSociety

Tenmaleand10femalesubjectswererecruitedviaadvertisementsinlocalmagazines. Criteriaforinclusionwere1)absenceofafamilyhistoryofdiabetes;2)age18–35yr;3)Caucasianrace;4)bodymassindex(BMI)20–25kg/m2;5)noexcessivesportactivities,i.e.fewerthanthreetimesperweek;and6)nomedication.
Afteran38hoffasting,measurementsofglucoseandlipidfluxesandmuscleceramideandfattyacidtranslocase/CD36wereperformedbeforeandafterahyperinsulinemiceuglycemicclamp
Ten male and 10 female subjects were recruited via advertisements in local magazines.
Criteria for inclusion were 1) absence of a family history of diabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr
4) body mass index (BMI) 20–25 kg/m2
the subjects were fasting from 2000 h 2 d before the start of the study until the end of the study
[6,6-2H]Glucose and [1,1,2,3,3-2H]glycerol were used as tracers (≥99% enriched; Cambridge Isotopes, Andover, MA).
To study total triglyceride hydrolysis, we used [1,1,2,3,3-2H5]glycerol.
infusions of insulin (60 mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; Novo Nordisk Farma B.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands)
Plasma glucose levels were measured every 5 min at the bedside.
At T = 8 h, five blood samples were drawn at 5-min intervals for determination of glucose and glycerol enrichments.
Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).

Oxygen consumption (VO2) and CO2 production (VCO2) were measured continuously during the final 20 min of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp by indirect calorimetry using a ventilated hood system
The muscle biopsy was performed under local anesthesia (lidocaine 20 mg/ml; Fresenius Kabi, Den Bosch, The Netherlands)
Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.
Plasma FFA concentrations were determined with an enzymatic colorimetric method (NEFA-C test kit; Wako Chemicals GmbH, Neuss, Germany)
Insulin was measured with a chemiluminescent immunometric assay with intraassay variation of 3–6%, interassay variation of 4–6%, and detection limit of 15 pmol/liter.
Cortisol was measured with a chemiluminescent immunoassay with intraassay variation of 7–8%, interassay variation of 7–8%, and detection limit of 50 nmol/liter.
Glucagon was determined with the Linco 125I RIA (Linco Research, St. Charles, MO) with an intraassay variation of 9–10%, interassay variation of 5–7%, and detection limit of 15 ng/liter.
Adiponectin was determined by a RIA (Linco) with intraassay variation of 2–7%, interassay variation of 16–17%, and detection limit of 1 ng/ml.
Ceramide and glucosylceramide in muscle biopsies were measured with an HPLC method as described (33).
Women were studied during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle.
For 3 d before the fasting period, all volunteers consumed a weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250 g carbohydrates per day.
saline was infused as NaCl 0.9% at a rate of 50 ml/h to keep the catheters patent.
[6,6-2H]glucose (prime, 8.8 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
[6,6-2H]glucose (prime, 8.8 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
[1,1,2,3,3-2H]glycerol (prime, 1.6 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
[1,1,2,3,3-2H]glycerol (prime, 1.6 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
After an equilibration period of 2 h (38 h of fasting), three blood samples were drawn for glucose and glycerol enrichments
glucose 20% (to maintain a plasma glucose level of 5 mmol/liter)
[6,6-2H]Glucose was added to the 20% glucose solution to achieve glucose enrichments of 1%

narrative_results.statement: Plasma glucose levels were significantly lower in women than men after 38 h of fasting

evidence.quote: Plasma glucose levels were significantly lower in women than men with a trend for a lower endogenous glucose production in women
In the female group, plasma glucose concentrations were significantly lower after 38 h of fasting compared with the male group

narrative_results.statement: Basal plasma FFA were significantly higher in women compared with men

evidence.quote: FFA and lipolysis were significantly higher
Basal plasma FFA were significantly higher in women compared with men but were equally suppressed during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp in both groups

narrative_results.statement: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes

evidence.quote: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes
Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake during the clamp (Rd) did not differ significantly between men and women

narrative_results.statement: There was no gender difference in muscle ceramide in the basal state

evidence.quote: There was no gender difference in muscle ceramide in the basal state
Muscle ceramide concentrations in the basal state were not different between sexes

narrative_results.statement: Ceramide did not correlate with peripheral glucose uptake

evidence.quote: ceramide did not correlate with peripheral glucose uptake
No correlation was found between insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake and muscle ceramide or glucosylceramide levels in women and men

narrative_results.statement: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp

evidence.quote: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp
There were no differences in muscle FAT/CD36 content between women and men during both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Adiponectin was significantly higher in females in both the basal state and during the clamp

evidence.quote: Adiponectin was significantly higher in females in both the basal state and during the clamp

narrative_results.statement: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

evidence.quote: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Women had significantly higher lipolysis rates in the basal state when expressed in micromoles per kilocalorie but not during the clamp

evidence.quote: when expressed in micromoles per kilocalorie, it was shown that women had significantly higher lipolysis rates in the basal state but not during the clamp

narrative_results.statement: Women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

evidence.quote: We suggest that during fasting, women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 describes glucose tracer type, Pass 2 describes glycerol tracer dose - different parameters entirely
Pass 1 describes glycerol tracer type, Pass 2 describes glycerol tracer continuous infusion dose - different parameters
Pass 1 describes basal sampling frequency, Pass 2 describes specific glucose/glycerol enrichment sampling at T=8h - different sampling protocols
Pass 1 describes enrichment sampling intervals, Pass 2 describes glucose tracer prime dose - completely different parameters
Pass 1 specifies only final 20 minutes. Pass 2 specifies two separate 20-minute periods (basal state and clamp). Different measurement protocols.
Pass_1 describes glucose prime dose (8.8 μmol/kg); pass_2 describes continuous infusion rate (0.11 μmol/kg·min). Different parameters and units indicate distinct measurements
Pass 1 describes glucose target level during clamp (5 mmol/liter); Pass 2 describes muscle biopsy timing - completely different parameters

semantic_groups.rationale: Identical age inclusion criteria with same formatting
Same BMI criteria, minor formatting difference in unit placement
Identical fasting duration specification
Pass 1 describes glucose tracer type, Pass 2 describes glycerol tracer dose - different parameters entirely
Pass 1 describes glycerol tracer type, Pass 2 describes glycerol tracer continuous infusion dose - different parameters
Same insulin infusion rate with minor formatting differences in unit presentation
Pass 1 describes basal sampling frequency, Pass 2 describes specific glucose/glycerol enrichment sampling at T=8h - different sampling protocols
Pass 1 describes enrichment sampling intervals, Pass 2 describes glucose tracer prime dose - completely different parameters
Both describe bioelectrical impedance analysis for body composition. Pass 1 specifies device model (Maltron BF906), Pass 2 is more generic. Same underlying fact.
Pass 1 specifies only final 20 minutes. Pass 2 specifies two separate 20-minute periods (basal state and clamp). Different measurement protocols.
Pass 1 contains muscle biopsy anesthesia information; Pass 2 is empty. Only one side present.
Both describe glucose oxidase method using Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2. Negligible wording differences in presentation.
Both describe enzymatic colorimetric method for FFA measurement. Pass 1 specifies NEFA-C kit, Pass 2 is more generic. Same underlying method.
Both describe chemiluminescent immunometric assay for insulin. Pass 1 specifies Immulite 2000 system, Pass 2 is generic. Same method.
Both describe chemiluminescent immunoassay for cortisol. Pass 1 specifies Immulite 2000 system, Pass 2 is generic. Same method.
Both describe Linco 125I RIA for glucagon measurement. Information presented in different order but identical content.
Both describe RIA method for adiponectin; pass_1 specifies manufacturer (Linco) while pass_2 is generic
Identical measurement method (HPLC) for ceramide and glucosylceramide
Both specify follicular phase of menstrual cycle
Pass_1 contains pre-study diet carbohydrate content information; pass_2 is empty
Pass_1 contains saline infusion rate information; pass_2 is empty
Pass_1 describes glucose prime dose (8.8 μmol/kg); pass_2 describes continuous infusion rate (0.11 μmol/kg·min). Different parameters and units indicate distinct measurements
Pass_1 contains glucose continuous infusion rate; pass_2 is empty
Pass_1 contains glycerol prime dose information; pass_2 is empty
Pass 1 contains glycerol infusion rate data; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 contains equilibration period duration; Pass 2 is empty
Pass 1 describes glucose target level during clamp (5 mmol/liter); Pass 2 describes muscle biopsy timing - completely different parameters
Pass 1 contains glucose enrichment target; Pass 2 is empty
Both describe identical participant composition: 10 male and 10 female subjects
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains catecholamine measurement method
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains sport activity inclusion criteria
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains race inclusion criteria
Identical statements about basal plasma FFA being significantly higher in women compared with men
Identical statements about insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake showing no sex difference
Identical statements about no gender difference in basal muscle ceramide
Identical statements about ceramide not correlating with peripheral glucose uptake
Identical statements about muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 showing no sex difference in basal state and during clamp
Identical statements about adiponectin being significantly higher in females in both basal state and during clamp
Identical statements about plasma epinephrine being significantly lower in females during hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

Both describe higher female lipolysis rates in basal state with different normalization methods (per kilocalorie vs per resting energy expenditure), which are related concepts. Pass 1 adds detail about clamp condition.
Both extractions are identical statements about ceramide accumulation protection in women
Same core finding about lower plasma glucose in women vs men; pass_1 includes specific fasting duration (38h) while pass_2 omits it
Pass_1 is empty; only pass_2 contains information about basal endogenous glucose production
